title: Can traceable cocoa deliver a fairer harvest?
dek: Ghanaian cooperatives are using simple digital records to strengthen farmers’ position in the supply chain.
author: Kwame Boateng
body: At a cooperative outside Kumasi, a small code connects each cocoa delivery to a farm, a date and a price paid.
Traceability is becoming a requirement in major markets, but farmers worry about carrying the cost of compliance. Cooperative ownership of the data offers a different route.
The system’s value will depend on whether transparency results in higher and more stable incomes—not merely better information for buyers.
